starter.expected: Target-shift, future-prefix invariance, and hard-gate ordering checks all print true.
starter.boundary: The starter contains no trained transformer, diffusion decoder, image encoder, or timing measurement.

buildOrder: Freeze token, frame, time, action, and target contracts
Pass future-token and corruption arithmetic tests
Overfit each decoder on one tiny batch
Restore checkpoints and verify next-step equivalence
Run matched decoder or sampler comparison

acceptanceChecks.action: Predict a0 at o0 and a1 at o1, then replace future action/instruction tokens.
acceptanceChecks.expected: Earlier logits remain invariant; padded keys are blocked and padded prediction rows contribute zero action loss.

acceptanceChecks.action: Reproduce one corruption and denoising update with declared shapes, scales, condition timestamp, and sampler calls.
acceptanceChecks.expected: Hand-computed values and tensor output agree before any rollout.

acceptanceChecks.action: Apply grounding, deadline, constraint, recovery, and assistance gates before success ranking.
acceptanceChecks.expected: A decoder failing any non-compensable gate is infeasible even if its aggregate task success is higher.

decision: Neither decoder fixture passes both the deadline and grounding gates, so aggregate success cannot select a winner; the implemented comparison must retain matched contracts, primitive budgets, and complete counterexample rows.
failure: The diffusion deadline miss and transformer word-swap grounding failure remain visible as non-compensable counterexamples with decoder revision, input condition, requested/applied action, and evaluator trace.
boundary: These values are teaching fixtures, not trained model quality, accelerator timing, simulator rollout measurements, physical-system results, or universal architecture rankings.
